articles.title: Researchers say we’re talking less than ever
articles.description:
Nobody is talking. | Image: Metrograph Pictures	

Researchers at the University of Missouri-Kansas City and the University of Arizona say that between 2005 and 2019, the number of words we speak out loud to another human being fell by nearly 28 percent. And that has likely only gotten worse following the pandemic.
The researchers actually counted the number of words we were speaking on average (16,632 in 2005). They looked at data from 22 studies in which over 2,000 people recorded audio of their daily lives. Over time, as ordering through apps became the norm, texting increased, and our lives became increasingly online, they found that number had dropped dramatically. By 2019, we were onl …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Sharge’s fast Qi2.2 MagSafe battery is down to $70 with a free USB-C cable
articles.description:
The Icemag 3’s kickstand allows you to prop up your phone as it recharges. | Image: Cameron Faulkner / The Verge	

I’ve been testing compact, magnetic Qi2 power banks that can snap onto your phone for an upcoming buying guide. They make recharging much easier than bringing along a huge battery that weighs down your bag. One of my favorites so far is the Sharge Icemag 3, a 10,000mAh model that can wirelessly output 25W to iPhone 16-series phones and newer models. It also offers a built-in kickstand and a 35W USB-C cable that doubles as a lanyard, plus a USB-C port for passthrough charging.
For a limited time, Amazon Prime subscribers can get it in either white or black for $69.90 ($10 off), and Sharge will include a basic 60W USB-C cable for free when you add both items to your cart. Alternatively, you can get the Icemag 3 for $2 less (sans the free cable) through Sharge’s own storefront. Using code ICE15 will knock it down to $67.92.
Sharge Icemag 3 Power Bank

Where to Buy:

 $79.9 $69.9 at Amazon (black, with Prime)
 $79.9 $69.9 at Amazon (white, with Prime)
 $79.9 $67.92 at Sharge (with code ICE15)

I’ll save my detailed charging speed test results for a later date, but I’m impressed so far with the Icemag 3. While it’s thicker than some other Qi2 power banks, the build quality is stellar, and it’s speedy when it comes to both wired and wireless charging. I also adore its see-through window, which showcases an LED-packed fan that keeps the battery cool while it recharges my gear. Plus, a free USB-C cable is a nice little gift.

articles.title: The Govee smart lamp brightened up my room, and then my life
articles.description:
I knew things were not quite right when I had to throw a towel over a broken Ikea lamp to block out its light. How did I get here? I cover fancy and capable tech for a living, and yet, it took me two years to get rid of a pair of old, broken Ikea lamps in my bedroom. Then I got some floor lamps from Govee that changed everything.
Those Ikea lamps were around for two years after I moved from Orange County to Los Angeles. Soon after that move, my mom's Parkinson's disease - a neurodegenerative condition with no cure - progressed quickly, my mental health took a hit, and most of my own to-do list quietly slid to the back burner as she lost mob …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Xbox’s weirdest studio is on a roll
articles.description:
For a while there, it seemed like Double Fine might be struggling under the Microsoft corporate umbrella. The game studio led by Tim Schafer is beloved for offbeat titles like Brütal Legend and Broken Age, but after being acquired by Microsoft in 2019, its only new release for years was a long-awaited sequel to Psychonauts. Of late, though, Double Fine is on something of a roll. Last year the studio released the wonderfully strange Keeper, a game about a sentient lighthouse. This week, it launched Kiln, a multiplayer brawler with adorable spirits and a whole lot of pottery. It's yet another oddball delight that could only come out of Double  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Sho Miyake answers life’s greatest questions
articles.description:
Acclaimed Japanese director Sho Miyake has arrived in the States. He's brought with him two feature films: Small, Slow But Steady and Two Seasons, Two Strangers, a pair of naturalistic portraits that deal with the uneasy human desire to relate to other people. Seclusion and unease are bedrocks to Miyake's growing filmography. "I like these characters that have a sense of discomfort that slowly starts to distance them from society," he tells The Verge.
I first saw Small, Slow But Steady at New Directors/New Films (lowkey one of the better film festivals New York has to offer). It's an affectionate story of a deaf boxer, Keiko (Yukino Kishii),  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: DOJ wants to shield its lawyers from outside scrutiny. Critics worry about oversight
articles.description: Critics say the proposed rule to let the DOJ step into state bar investigations could weaken one of the last independent checks on government lawyers.

articles.title: The rising cost of fertilizer and fuel prices is pushing some farmers to the brink
articles.description: In the Mississippi Delta, a crucial agricultural region, farmers say their patience is wearing thin. Reeling from the effects of tariffs, they must now also navigate rising fertilizer and fuel costs.

articles.title: Some Planned Parenthood clinics are using Botox to smooth over federal cuts
articles.description: After President Trump and Congress cut certain Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood in last year's budget, some clinics have started offering aesthetic services, including Botox, to stay afloat.

articles.title: L&T Finance Q4 profit climbs 27 pc to Rs 807 cr
articles.description: L&T Finance announced a significant 27 percent jump in its March quarter profit after tax, reaching Rs 807 crore. This growth was fueled by a sharp increase in interest income and robust retail loan disbursements. The company's core net interest also saw an upward trend.

articles.title: Smallcaps back in favour as valuations turn attractive: Siddharth Vora
articles.description: Siddharth Vora has turned constructive on the market, seeing attractive valuations after a recent correction, and is increasing exposure to smallcaps. His portfolio remains overweight in metals, power, and materials, while avoiding IT. He sees contrarian opportunities in oil marketing companies after exiting ONGC.

articles.title: On Iran’s Border, Cooking Oil Trade Is a Snapshot of the Country’s Struggles
articles.description: The war has exacerbated Iran’s economic crisis, forcing many to cross the border into Turkey to buy the most basic goods.

articles.title: Welsh fire, England captain - how Meg Jones became a dual-nation icon
articles.description: Swapping Welsh roots for the England captaincy could be enough to turn a nation against you. But Meg Jones is different.

articles.description: Both words are being deployed by multiple sides at once as Raghav Chadha caused the latest breakup in the party currently ruling Punjab.

articles.title: China's next aircraft carrier to be nuclear-powered ‘He Jian’? New video sparks buzz
articles.description: China's Navy released a seven-minute video, creating buzz on social media about its next aircraft carrier. Many believe that the video offers a hint that the next carrier is likely to be “nuclear-powered.”

articles.description: Punjab Kings skipper Shreyas Iyer continued his incredible chasing record during his side's IPL 2026 match against Delhi Capitals.

articles.description: Ashok Kumar Lahiri, a former Chief Economic Advisor to the government, replaces Suman K. Bery

topStories.title: Rep. Luna calls for pardon of Special Forces soldier accused of betting on his own mission to capture Maduro
topStories.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna called for a pardon for a Special Forces soldier charged after betting on his mission, citing a double standard with Congress.

topStories.title: Mom who got 2-day sentence after killing baby while high arrested for endangering someone else's child
topStories.description: Pennsylvania mother Arissa Ward faces felony child endangerment charges after a toddler she was babysitting was allegedly found wandering in the road.

topStories.title: US man stole colleague’s identity for 30 years, victim imprisoned and forced into mental hospital
topStories.description: A US federal appeals court has upheld the 12-year prison sentence of Matthew David Keirans, who impersonated his coworker for over three decades—an act that led to the wrongful arrest and forced institutionalisation of William Woods.

topStories.title: US to allow Venezuelan government to cover Maduro’s lawyer fees
topStories.description: Defence lawyers had asked for case to be thrown out, claiming Maduro's rights were violated following US abduction.

topStories.title: FLASHBACK: Obama tried to make Trump a punchline at 2011 dinner before rise stunned Washington
topStories.description: President Trump attends the White House Correspondents' Dinner for the first time as commander-in-chief after being roasted by Obama in 2011.

topStories.title: Trump Says He Has Cancelled US Officials' Trip To Pakistan For Iran Talks
topStories.description: Trump says he has cancelled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner's trip to Pak for Iran talks: News agency AFP citing Fox News

topStories.title: No more 18-hour flights for Iran talks: Trump scraps envoys' Pak trip, says US holds all the cards
topStories.description: No more hour flights for Iran talks Trump scraps envoys Pak trip says US holds all the cards

topStories.title: Elon Musk's case against OpenAI, Sam Altman set to go for trial
topStories.description: A US judge has dismissed Elon Musk's fraud claims in his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman. The case will now proceed to trial on Musk's claims of breach of charitable trust and unjust enrichment. Jury selection begins Monday, with opening arguments Tuesday. Musk seeks $150 billion in damages for OpenAI's charitable arm.

topStories.title: Billion-Barrel Hormuz Oil Shock Is About to Crash Demand
topStories.description: The Strait of Hormuz oil shock has yet to crash demand as the rich world borrows from its stocks and pays up to secure supply. Traders are now sounding the alarm that a harsh adjustment is coming. Bloomberg News Washington Bureau Chief Peggy Collins and White House Correspondent Jeff Mason join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend for a wide-ranging conversation on the latest in politics. (Source: Bloomberg)

topStories.title: Nation’s second largest school district moves to limit kids’ screen time
topStories.description: Los Angeles Unified School District passed a resolution to limit student laptop and tablet use in classrooms and incentivize pen-and-paper assignments.

topStories.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
topStories.description: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

topStories.title: Ne-Yo embraces country’s everyday lifestyle, says R&B’s ‘sexiest guy, all the money’ image isn’t reality
topStories.description: Ne-Yo says Nashville welcomed him with open arms despite warnings from industry friends, and he's now working on a new country-inspired album in Music City.

dailyNews.title: WWE legend Booker T hopes latest ‘WWE LFG’ competitors understand one big thing about pro wrestling
dailyNews.description: WWE Hall of Famer Booker T returns to coach pro wrestling hopefuls in "WWE LFG" Season 3, where it's every man or woman for themselves on A&E this Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Chef Nick Kennedy on creating a dining destination in Philadelphia's Fishtown neighborhood
dailyNews.description: Chef Nick Kennedy felt a pull to create a more robust dining community in a small Philadelphia neighborhood. His solution was Defined Hospitality, a restaurant group that owns seven bars and restaurants in Fishtown.

dailyNews.title: ‘End the cover-up’: Trump and Epstein videos projected on Washington hotel building ahead of White House press dinner
dailyNews.description: Protesters in Washington, DC projected images related to Donald Trump and Jeffrey Epstein onto the Washington Hilton ahead of the White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner. The display included photographs, documents, and audio readings of Epstein's emails, attracting a crowd of onlookers.

dailyNews.title: Book excerpt: "The First Eight" by Jim Clyburn
dailyNews.description: The South Carolina Democrat, the ninth Black man to represent his state in the House of Representatives, writes of his predecessors who helped direct the course of America during and after Reconstruction.

dailyNews.title: Frankie Grande on starring in "Titaníque"
dailyNews.description: Frankie Grande sits down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to discuss his starring role in Broadway's latest hit show "Titaníque," after originating the part in the show's Off-Broadway run.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  Make-A-Wish recipient now helping children’s dreams come true
dailyNews.description: Abigail Hoyle's journey with Disney and the Make-A-Wish Foundation began 15 years ago and now continues as she helps children experience the same joy as a cast member at the parks.

dailyNews.title: Triumphal Arch: How Trump is Leaving His Mark on DC
dailyNews.description: Twice the size of the Lincoln Memorial, taller than almost all structures in Washington DC, and the winged figure atop rising some 60 feet high, the equivalent of a six-story building: President Donald Trump’s proposed “Triumphal Arch.” Bloomberg CityLab writer Kriston Capps joins Christina Ruffini in Washington DC to discuss the impact Trump is having on the architecture of the nation's capital. (Source: Bloomberg)

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  Remote worker paraglides on the job
dailyNews.description: Scott Muirhead, a content creator, staged a Zoom call while paragliding off the coast of San Diego.

dailyNews.description: Rep. Michael McCaul (R-TX) joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the US-Iranian talks that were meant to end the conflict that has closed the Strait of Hormuz.  (Source: Bloomberg)

dailyNews.description: The map still favors Republicans, but Trump’s declining popularity — particularly on the economy — makes Democrats’ once long-shot bid to retake the Senate more feasible than ever before.

dailyNews.description: President Trump withdrew from the Obama-era nuclear accord in 2018, saying it was the worst deal ever. But Iran responded with an enrichment spree that haunts the negotiations to this day.

dailyNews.title: Firm Building Trump’s Ballroom Got a Secret No-Bid Contract for a Nearby Job
dailyNews.description: The National Park Service increased the value of the contract several times over and then awarded it to Maryland-based Clark Construction, in a process that experts said was highly unusual.

dailyNews.title: Mike Tyson, Trump watches and meme coins: Crypto takes over Mar-a-Lago
dailyNews.description: Top holders of Donald Trump’s meme coin will get a conference at Mar-a-Lago, featuring Trump-branded swag and potential facetime, even as the coin has lost value.

dailyNews.title: Trump eager to show off latest White House project to King Charles
dailyNews.description: The president’s black granite path is the newest change to the West Wing, following gold lettering, Oval Office gilding and a “Presidential Walk of Fame.”

dailyNews.description: The SPLC allegedly funded the very racist organizations it monitored, and a DOJ grand jury indictment now exposes decades of apparent deception.

dailyNews.title: Trump to headline 2026 White House Correspondents' Dinner for the first time as president
dailyNews.description: President Trump is attending the White House Correspondents’ Dinner Saturday for the first time as commander-in-chief—after boycotting the annual event last year and each year during his first term.
